source_dataset: IMF - 'Islamic Republic of Iran -- 2002 Article IV Consultation, Concluding Statement of the IMF Mission'
notes: IMF mission visited Tehran 26 June-11 July 2002. Mission commended Iranian authorities for the March 2002 exchange-rate unification (merging the official and parallel/market rates into a single managed rate) and the smooth transition, which enhanced business confidence and reform credibility.

source_dataset: IMF - 'Statement by IMF 2004 Article IV Consultation Mission to the Islamic Republic of Iran' / IMF Country Report 04/308
original_period_label: 2004 (2004 (mission, exact date not captured this round))
notes: Mission led by Abdelali Jbili (Assistant Director, Middle East and Central Asia Dept). Findings: real GDP growth high and broad-based in 2003-04; unemployment declined; gross international reserves rose to over 6 months of import cover; external current-account surplus narrowed vs. prior year; inflation ~15%.
Executive Board concluded the 2006 Article IV Consultation with Iran (headline findings not captured in this round's search snippets -- see PN for full text).
Executive Board concluded the 2011 Article IV Consultation with Iran on 20 July 2011 (Public Information Notice 11/107).

source_dataset: IMF - Press Release 14/152 / mission statement PR 14/50
notes: Executive Board concluded the 2014 Article IV Consultation with Iran on 28 March 2014.

Executive Board concluded the 2015 Article IV Consultation with Iran on 7 December 2015 -- notable as the consultation immediately following the JCPOA nuclear deal (finalized 14 July 2015, ahead of Jan 2016 Implementation Day sanctions relief).
Executive Board concluded the 2016 Article IV Consultation with Iran in late February 2017 -- the first full post-JCPOA-implementation review, assessing the initial sanctions-relief growth rebound.

source_dataset: IMF - Press Release 18/114 / IMF Country Report 18/93
notes: Executive Board concluded the 2018 Article IV Consultation with Iran -- the LAST full Article IV consultation completed with Iran as of this round's research (mid-2026), a 7+ year gap follows.

source_dataset: IMF - 'Islamic Republic of Iran and the IMF' country page + informal-briefing press release (below)
notes: No full Article IV Consultation with Iran was completed for calendar/consultation years 2019 through 2024 -- the longest gap in Iran's IMF consultation history captured in this dataset. Consistent with the IMF's standard procedure for members with delayed consultations (often due to sanctions-related data-access or scheduling constraints), Iran received only informal staff monitoring during this period rather than a formal Board-concluded consultation.

variant_label: Informal Executive Board briefing (not a full Article IV conclusion)
source_dataset: IMF - Press Release 25/65 'IMF Holds Informal Board Briefing on Afghanistan, Iran, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia, and Yemen'
notes: IMF Executive Board received an INFORMAL staff briefing on economic developments in Iran on 14 March 2025 -- grouped together with Afghanistan, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia, and Yemen, i.e. the IMF's standard cohort of members whose Article IV consultations are significantly delayed. This is explicitly NOT a full Article IV consultation conclusion, underscoring that Iran's formal consultation cycle has been suspended/delayed since 2018.
